title: Machine Needs Descaling
shortDescription: The machine requires descaling to remove mineral buildup.
fullDescription: A steady orange light on Nespresso Vertuo and Original line machines indicates the descaling alert has been triggered. The internal counter has determined enough cups have been brewed to warrant descaling. On Vertuo machines, the light turns steady orange. On Original machines, both buttons may flash orange.

causes.cause: Normal mineral buildup from regular use
Hard water causing faster scale accumulation
Previous descaling cycle not completed
Water filter expired or not installed

symptoms: Steady orange light on Vertuo machines
Both buttons flash on Original line machines
Coffee brewing slower than usual
Coffee temperature lower than normal
Reduced coffee volume per brew

fixSteps.title: Prepare descaling solution
fixSteps.instruction: Empty the drip tray and capsule container. Fill the water tank with 500ml of fresh water and add one packet of Nespresso descaling solution.

fixSteps.title: Enter descaling mode
fixSteps.instruction: For Vertuo: With machine off, hold the button for 7 seconds until the light flashes rapidly. For Original: Press and hold both buttons for 3 seconds.

fixSteps.title: Run descaling cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Place a large container under the spout. Press the button to start the cycle. The machine will dispense the solution in intervals. The full cycle takes about 20 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Rinse cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Empty and rinse the water tank. Fill with fresh water and run the rinse cycle to flush all descaling solution from the system. Repeat if the water tastes acidic.

whenToCallPro: Only if the descaling alert does not clear after completing the full descaling and rinse cycle.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso descaling how to steady orange light fix

faq.q: How often should I descale my Nespresso?
faq.a: Nespresso recommends every 300 capsules or every 3 months, whichever comes first.

faq.q: Can I use vinegar to descale?
faq.a: Nespresso advises against using vinegar as it can damage seals and leave a lingering taste. Use the official Nespresso descaling solution.

faq.q: What happens if I ignore the descaling alert?
faq.a: Scale buildup will reduce water flow, lower brew temperature, and eventually block the system completely.

title: Machine Error or General Fault
shortDescription: The machine has detected a general error requiring attention.
fullDescription: A flashing or pulsing orange light indicates a general error. On Vertuo machines, different flash patterns have different meanings: 2 flashes means the head is not properly locked, 3 flashes indicates the machine needs cleaning, fast flashing indicates overheating.

causes.cause: Brew head not properly closed or locked
Machine has overheated from continuous use
Internal blockage in the brewing chamber
Motor or pump fault

symptoms: Orange light flashing in various patterns
Machine refuses to brew
Brew head feels stuck or will not lock
Machine is hot to the touch

fixSteps.title: Check the brew head
fixSteps.instruction: For Vertuo: Ensure the head is fully closed and locked. Lift and firmly close the head until it clicks. For Original: Ensure the lever is fully locked down.

fixSteps.title: Let the machine cool down
fixSteps.instruction: If the machine has been used heavily, unplug it and wait 15-20 minutes for it to cool before retrying.

fixSteps.title: Clean the brewing chamber
fixSteps.instruction: Open the head and inspect the brewing chamber for stuck capsule fragments or coffee residue. Clean with a damp cloth.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: For Vertuo: Open the head, press the button 5 times within 3 seconds, close the head. For Original: consult model-specific instructions.

whenToCallPro: If the flashing persists after cleaning, cooling, and resetting.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso flashing orange light fix reset

faq.q: What do different Nespresso flash patterns mean?
faq.a: 2 flashes = head not locked. 3 flashes = cleaning needed. Fast continuous = overheating. Slow pulsing = general error.

faq.q: How do I factory reset my Nespresso Vertuo?
faq.a: Open the head, press the button 5 times within 3 seconds, then close the head. The light will blink 5 times to confirm.

faq.q: Why does my Nespresso keep overheating?
faq.a: Brewing many cups in quick succession can cause overheating. Allow a few minutes between consecutive brews.

title: Critical Error or Machine Malfunction
shortDescription: A red light indicates a serious error that prevents the machine from operating.
fullDescription: A red light on Nespresso machines indicates a critical error. On Vertuo machines, a solid red means the machine has detected an unrecoverable fault. On Original machines, a red light may indicate the water tank is empty or a critical internal error.

causes.cause: Water tank empty or not detected
Capsule jammed in the brewing chamber
Internal leak detected by safety sensor
Pump or motor failure

symptoms: Solid red or flashing red light
Machine completely unresponsive
No water flows when brewing
Unusual noise followed by red light

fixSteps.title: Check the water tank
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the water tank is filled and properly seated. Remove and reinsert the tank firmly.

fixSteps.title: Check for jammed capsule
fixSteps.instruction: Open the brew head and inspect for a stuck or crushed capsule. Remove any capsule remnants carefully.

fixSteps.title: Perform a hard reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 15 minutes. Plug it back in and try turning on. This resets the internal controller.

fixSteps.title: Contact Nespresso support
fixSteps.instruction: If the red light persists, the machine may have an internal leak or motor failure. Contact Nespresso Club for repair or replacement options.

whenToCallPro: If the red light persists after checking water, capsule, and performing a hard reset.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso red light error fix hard reset

faq.q: Does a red light mean my Nespresso is broken?
faq.a: Not always. Start with simple checks like the water tank and capsule chamber. Many red light issues are easily resolved.

faq.q: Is Nespresso repair worth the cost?
faq.a: Depending on the model's age and cost, it may be more economical to use the Nespresso warranty or purchase a new machine.

faq.q: How long is the Nespresso warranty?
faq.a: Nespresso machines come with a 2-year warranty. Extended care plans may be available through Nespresso Club.

title: Special Function or Update Mode
shortDescription: The machine is in a special function mode such as firmware update or error state.
fullDescription: On Vertuo Next and newer models, a light that alternates between red and green or shows both colors indicates the machine is performing a firmware update via Bluetooth or has entered a special diagnostic mode.

causes.cause: Firmware update in progress via Bluetooth
Machine stuck in diagnostic mode
Bluetooth pairing issue during update
Firmware corruption

symptoms: Light alternates between red and green
Machine does not respond to button presses
Occurs after connecting to Nespresso app
Machine was updating when power was lost

fixSteps.title: Wait for the update to complete
fixSteps.instruction: If a firmware update is in progress, do not unplug the machine. Wait up to 15 minutes for it to complete.

fixSteps.title: Ensure Bluetooth is connected
fixSteps.instruction: Keep the Nespresso app open and your phone near the machine with Bluetooth enabled during updates.

fixSteps.title: Hard reset if stuck
fixSteps.instruction: If the update appears stuck for more than 20 minutes, unplug the machine for 30 seconds and plug it back in.

fixSteps.title: Retry the update
fixSteps.instruction: Open the Nespresso app and retry the firmware update if it failed.

whenToCallPro: If the machine is stuck in update mode and cannot be recovered with a hard reset.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso Vertuo Next half red green light firmware update fix

faq.q: Which Nespresso machines have Bluetooth?
faq.a: Vertuo Next and Vertuo Pop have Bluetooth for firmware updates and connectivity with the Nespresso app.

faq.q: Is a firmware update mandatory?
faq.a: Not mandatory, but recommended for bug fixes and performance improvements.

faq.q: What if the update fails?
faq.a: A hard reset (unplug 30 seconds) usually allows you to retry the update.

title: No Water Flowing During Brew
shortDescription: The machine pumps but no water or coffee comes out of the spout.
fullDescription: The pump motor runs and the machine seems to be operating, but no liquid flows from the spout. This is typically caused by an air lock in the pump, a blocked system, or severe scale buildup.

causes.cause: Air lock in the pump system
Severe scale buildup blocking water flow
Capsule not pierced properly
Pump failure

symptoms: Pump sounds run but nothing comes out
Very slow drip instead of normal flow
Machine vibrates more than usual
Coffee comes out very thin or watery

fixSteps.title: Prime the pump to remove air lock
fixSteps.instruction: Fill the water tank. Run the machine without a capsule to push water through the system. On Vertuo: close the head empty and press brew. On Original: lift and close the lever and press a button.

fixSteps.title: Run hot water cycles
fixSteps.instruction: Run 3-5 cycles of hot water without a capsule to clear any minor blockages.

fixSteps.title: Descale the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Heavy scale buildup can completely block water flow. Run a full descaling cycle with Nespresso solution.

fixSteps.title: Check the capsule area
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the capsule chamber for a stuck capsule that might be blocking the water inlet or outlet needle.

whenToCallPro: If no water flows even after descaling and multiple priming attempts.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso no water flow fix air lock prime pump

faq.q: What causes an air lock in Nespresso?
faq.a: Running the machine with an empty water tank can introduce air into the pump. Always keep the tank filled.

faq.q: How do I prime the Nespresso pump?
faq.a: Fill the tank, close the head without a capsule, and press the brew button. Repeat 3-5 times until water flows normally.

faq.q: Can I use a needle to clear the nozzle?
faq.a: Do not insert objects into the brew nozzle. Running water cycles and descaling are the proper methods.

title: Machine Leaking Water
shortDescription: Water is leaking from the machine body, drip tray area, or capsule chamber.
fullDescription: Water leaking from a Nespresso machine can originate from multiple sources: the capsule chamber seal, the water tank connection, the drip tray overflow, or an internal hose connection.

causes.cause: Drip tray is full and overflowing
Capsule chamber seal worn or damaged
Water tank not properly seated
Internal hose connection loose or cracked

symptoms: Water pooling under the machine
Water dripping from the capsule area after brewing
Drip tray fills very quickly
Water tank area is wet

fixSteps.title: Empty the drip tray
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and empty the drip tray. Check if it was simply overfull.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the water tank
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water tank, dry the connection area, and reinsert it firmly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the capsule area
fixSteps.instruction: Open the brew head and check the rubber seals around the capsule chamber for wear, cracks, or coffee residue buildup that prevents sealing.

fixSteps.title: Clean capsule chamber seals
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the rubber seals with a damp cloth. Remove any coffee residue or scale that could prevent a proper seal.

whenToCallPro: If water is leaking from inside the body (not the capsule area or drip tray), indicating an internal hose issue.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso leaking water fix seal replacement

faq.q: Is a small amount of water in the drip tray normal?
faq.a: Yes, Nespresso machines drain residual water into the drip tray after each brew. This is normal.

faq.q: How often should I empty the drip tray?
faq.a: Empty it daily or when the float indicator rises to the top.

faq.q: Can coffee residue cause leaks?
faq.a: Yes, buildup on the capsule chamber seals can prevent proper sealing, causing leaks during brewing.

title: Vertuo Capsule Not Recognized
shortDescription: The Vertuo machine cannot read the barcode on the capsule.
fullDescription: Vertuo machines read barcodes on capsules to determine the brew parameters (water volume, temperature, spin speed). When the barcode cannot be read, the machine will flash an error or refuse to brew.

causes.cause: Capsule inserted incorrectly
Barcode on capsule damaged or dirty
Barcode reader lens dirty
Non-genuine capsule without proper barcode

symptoms: Machine flashes error after capsule insertion
Red or orange light after inserting capsule
Machine starts but brews incorrect volume
Error only with certain capsules

fixSteps.title: Reinsert the capsule
fixSteps.instruction: Open the head, remove the capsule, and reinsert it with the flat side up and the dome facing down into the capsule chamber.

fixSteps.title: Check the capsule barcode
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the barcode ring on the edge of the capsule for damage, scratches, or residue that could prevent reading.

fixSteps.title: Clean the barcode reader
fixSteps.instruction: Open the head and locate the barcode reader lens inside the brew chamber. Wipe it gently with a dry cotton swab.

fixSteps.title: Try a different capsule
fixSteps.instruction: Test with a different Nespresso capsule. If some capsules work, the issue is with specific capsules rather than the machine.

whenToCallPro: If the barcode reader hardware has failed and no capsules are recognized.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso Vertuo capsule not recognized barcode fix

faq.q: Can I use third-party capsules in Vertuo?
faq.a: Third-party Vertuo-compatible capsules must have the correct barcode. Not all third-party capsules include it.

faq.q: Does Original line use barcodes?
faq.a: No, Original line machines do not use barcodes. You manually select the brew size.

faq.q: What does the barcode tell the machine?
faq.a: The barcode encodes the brew recipe: water volume, temperature, flow rate, and centrifugal spin speed.

title: Milk Frothing System Not Working (Lattissima)
shortDescription: The automatic milk frothing system is not producing froth or dispensing milk.
fullDescription: On Nespresso Lattissima models with built-in milk frothers, this error indicates the milk system cannot froth or dispense milk. The milk circuit may be blocked, the frothing chamber dirty, or the milk carafe not properly connected.

causes.cause: Milk residue blocking the frothing circuit
Milk carafe not properly connected
Frothing nozzle clogged with dried milk
Milk pump failure

symptoms: No milk dispensed during cappuccino or latte
Milk comes out without froth
Milk dispenses very slowly
Steam comes out instead of frothed milk

fixSteps.title: Clean the milk system
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the milk carafe and disassemble the frothing components. Wash all parts in warm soapy water. Use a thin brush to clean the milk tube.

fixSteps.title: Run the milk system clean cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Fill the carafe with water (no milk) and run the clean/steam function to flush the milk circuit.

fixSteps.title: Check carafe connection
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the milk carafe clicks firmly into place and the spout is properly aligned under the dispensing area.

fixSteps.title: Soak stubborn residue
fixSteps.instruction: If blockage persists, soak the frothing components in warm water with a drop of dish soap for 30 minutes, then rinse thoroughly.

whenToCallPro: If the milk pump has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso Lattissima milk system not working clean fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the milk system?
faq.a: After every use. Milk residue builds up quickly and becomes difficult to remove if left overnight.

faq.q: Can I use plant-based milk?
faq.a: Yes, but plant-based milks may froth differently. Oat and soy milk tend to froth best.

faq.q: Why is my milk not frothy?
faq.a: The frothing nozzle may be clogged. Clean all components and ensure the air intake is not blocked.

title: Machine Heating Up
shortDescription: The machine is in the warm-up phase and not yet ready to brew.
fullDescription: A rotating or pulsing white light indicates the machine is heating up. This is normal after power-on and should complete within 15-30 seconds. If it continues for much longer, there may be a heating issue.

causes.cause: Normal warm-up cycle after power-on
Heating element taking longer due to cold environment
Scale buildup in the thermoblock reducing heating efficiency
Thermoblock or heating element failure

symptoms: White light spins or pulses after turning on
Machine takes more than 2 minutes to heat up
Machine never reaches ready state
Coffee comes out lukewarm

fixSteps.title: Wait for normal warm-up
fixSteps.instruction: After turning on, wait 15-30 seconds for the machine to reach brewing temperature. This is the normal warm-up time.

fixSteps.title: Check room temperature
fixSteps.instruction: In very cold environments, the machine may take longer to heat. Place it in a warmer area.

fixSteps.title: Descale if heating is slow
fixSteps.instruction: Scale buildup insulates the thermoblock, making it less efficient. Run a descaling cycle.

fixSteps.title: Contact support if stuck
fixSteps.instruction: If the machine never reaches ready (white light does not stop rotating after 5 minutes), the heating element may have failed.

whenToCallPro: If the machine never completes heating after 5+ minutes.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso Vertuo spinning white light not ready fix

faq.q: How long should Nespresso take to heat up?
faq.a: Typically 15-30 seconds for Vertuo and 20-25 seconds for Original line machines.

faq.q: Why does my Nespresso take so long to heat up?
faq.a: Scale buildup or a cold environment can slow heating. Descaling usually resolves extended warm-up times.

faq.q: Is it bad to turn my Nespresso off between uses?
faq.a: Modern Nespresso machines have fast heat-up times, so turning off between uses is fine and saves energy.

title: Coffee Volume Smaller Than Expected
shortDescription: The machine dispenses less coffee than the selected cup size.
fullDescription: The machine starts brewing but produces significantly less liquid than expected for the selected cup size. This can be caused by scale buildup restricting flow, air in the system, or incorrect programming.

causes.cause: Scale buildup restricting water flow
Cup size setting was changed accidentally
Air lock partially blocking the pump
Worn pump not generating enough pressure

symptoms: Coffee cup is only half full
Brew takes longer than usual but less liquid comes out
Coffee tastes stronger than normal
Machine sounds strained during brewing

fixSteps.title: Reprogram cup sizes
fixSteps.instruction: For Original: Hold the desired cup size button while brewing to reprogram the water volume. For Vertuo: cup sizes are capsule-determined.

fixSteps.title: Descale the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Scale buildup is the most common cause. Run a full descaling cycle.

fixSteps.title: Prime the pump
fixSteps.instruction: Run 2-3 cycles without a capsule to flush air from the system.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: Reset cup volumes to factory defaults and try again.

whenToCallPro: If the volume remains low after descaling and priming.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso coffee too short small cup fix descale

faq.q: Can I change the cup size on Vertuo?
faq.a: No, Vertuo cup sizes are determined by the capsule barcode and cannot be manually adjusted.

faq.q: How do I reprogram Nespresso Original cup sizes?
faq.a: Press and hold the desired cup size button while the machine brews. Release when the cup reaches your desired volume.

faq.q: Why does my Nespresso make less coffee over time?
faq.a: Scale buildup gradually restricts water flow. Regular descaling prevents this.

title: Machine in Standby or Energy-Saving Mode
shortDescription: The green flashing light indicates the machine is entering or in energy-saving standby mode.
fullDescription: Flashing green means the machine is entering standby or auto-off mode. This is normal behavior designed to save energy. The machine will power down completely after a set time.

causes.cause: Auto-off timer activated (normal behavior)
Energy-saving mode enabled in settings
Machine inactivity for the set timeout period
Power button pressed briefly

symptoms: Green light flashes before machine turns off
Machine turns off after period of inactivity
Machine will not stay on indefinitely
Green flashing followed by all lights off

fixSteps.title: Press the button to wake
fixSteps.instruction: Press the main button to wake the machine from standby. It will begin heating up.

fixSteps.title: Adjust auto-off timer
fixSteps.instruction: For Original: Press and hold the two cup buttons simultaneously for 3 seconds to cycle through auto-off settings (9, 30 minutes, or off).

fixSteps.title: Check MyDyson app settings
fixSteps.instruction: For Vertuo Next/Pop with Bluetooth, adjust the auto-off timer in the Nespresso app.

fixSteps.title: Accept as normal
fixSteps.instruction: The flashing green is a normal power-saving feature and is not an error.

whenToCallPro: This is normal behavior. No professional service needed.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso flashing green light standby auto-off setting

faq.q: Is the flashing green light an error?
faq.a: No, it indicates the machine is entering energy-saving standby mode, which is normal.

faq.q: Can I disable auto-off?
faq.a: On most Original line machines, yes. Hold both cup buttons for 3 seconds to adjust. Vertuo models may not allow disabling.

faq.q: How long before Nespresso turns off automatically?
faq.a: Default is typically 9 minutes. Can be set to 30 minutes on some models.

title: Capsule Jammed in Brew Chamber
shortDescription: A capsule is stuck in the brewing chamber and cannot be ejected.
fullDescription: A used or new capsule has become stuck in the brewing chamber. The head may not open or close properly, the capsule may not eject into the used capsule container, or a capsule fragment is lodged in the piercing mechanism.

causes.cause: Capsule expanded during brewing and is stuck
Multiple capsules inserted simultaneously
Capsule container is full preventing ejection
Ejection mechanism jammed

symptoms: Cannot close or open the brew head
Capsule does not eject when head is opened
Machine makes unusual noise when trying to eject
Multiple capsules jammed together

fixSteps.title: Empty the capsule container
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and empty the used capsule container. A full container prevents ejection of new capsules.

fixSteps.title: Manually remove the stuck capsule
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine. Open the head and use a spoon or butter knife to gently pry the stuck capsule out. Do not use sharp objects that could damage the seals.

fixSteps.title: Clean the capsule chamber
fixSteps.instruction: After removing the stuck capsule, wipe the chamber with a damp cloth to remove coffee residue.

fixSteps.title: Test the ejection mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Without a capsule, open and close the head several times to ensure the ejection mechanism moves freely.

whenToCallPro: If the ejection mechanism is physically broken.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso capsule stuck jammed fix remove

faq.q: Can I put two capsules in at once?
faq.a: Never. One capsule at a time. The previous capsule must be ejected before inserting a new one.

faq.q: Why do Vertuo capsules swell?
faq.a: The centrifugal brewing process causes Vertuo capsules to expand slightly. This is normal and they should still eject.

faq.q: How often should I empty the capsule container?
faq.a: After every 10-12 capsules or when the container is full.

title: Coffee Brewing at Low Temperature
shortDescription: The coffee comes out at a lower temperature than expected.
fullDescription: The coffee is dispensed at a noticeably lower temperature than normal. This is typically caused by scale buildup insulating the thermoblock, a cold cup, or a machine that has not fully heated up.

causes.cause: Machine not fully heated before brewing
Scale buildup reducing thermoblock efficiency
Cold cup absorbing heat from coffee
Thermoblock or temperature sensor failure

symptoms: Coffee feels lukewarm to the touch
First cup of the day is cold but subsequent cups are warmer
Coffee temperature has gradually decreased over weeks
Machine makes unusual gurgling sounds

fixSteps.title: Pre-heat the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Let the machine fully heat up (15-30 seconds) before brewing. Run a hot water cycle first to warm the internal pathways.

fixSteps.title: Pre-heat the cup
fixSteps.instruction: Run hot water from the machine into your cup first, then discard and brew. This prevents the cold cup from absorbing heat.

fixSteps.title: Descale the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Scale insulates the thermoblock, reducing heating efficiency. Descale the machine following the standard procedure.

fixSteps.title: Check for Vertuo temperature settings
fixSteps.instruction: Some Vertuo models allow temperature adjustment in the Nespresso app. Check that it is not set to a lower temperature.

whenToCallPro: If temperature remains low after descaling with the machine fully heated.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso coffee too cold low temperature fix descale

faq.q: What temperature should Nespresso coffee be?
faq.a: Nespresso machines brew at approximately 83-86C (181-187F). The cup temperature will be slightly lower.

faq.q: Why is the first cup always colder?
faq.a: The machine and internal pathways are cold. Running a hot water cycle first warms everything up.

faq.q: Can I make my Nespresso hotter?
faq.a: Pre-heating the cup and running a water cycle first helps. Some Vertuo models have app-based temperature settings.

title: Aeroccino Milk Frother Malfunction
shortDescription: The standalone Aeroccino milk frother is not frothing or heating milk.
fullDescription: The Nespresso Aeroccino standalone milk frother is not producing froth, not heating milk, or displaying a red light. This is separate from built-in Lattissima frothing systems.

causes.cause: Milk residue on the whisk or inside the jug
Wrong whisk installed for desired function
Overfilled beyond the maximum line
Base plate connection issue

symptoms: Frother turns on but does not froth
Milk heats but no foam produced
Red light blinks and unit shuts off
Frother does not power on

fixSteps.title: Clean the jug and whisk
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the whisk and wash both the whisk and inside of the jug with warm soapy water. Dried milk on the whisk magnet prevents it from spinning.

fixSteps.title: Use the correct whisk
fixSteps.instruction: Aeroccino comes with two whisks: one with a spring coil (for frothing) and one without (for heating only). Use the coiled whisk for froth.

fixSteps.title: Check milk level
fixSteps.instruction: Do not exceed the MAX line. For frothing, fill only to the lower MIN line. Overfilling prevents proper frothing.

fixSteps.title: Ensure proper contact with base
fixSteps.instruction: Place the jug firmly on the base plate. The electrical contacts on the bottom must align with the base.

whenToCallPro: If the base heating element or motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Nespresso Aeroccino not frothing fix whisk cleaning

faq.q: Why is my Aeroccino not making froth?
faq.a: Usually a dirty whisk. The magnetic whisk needs to spin freely. Clean it thoroughly and use the frothing whisk (with spring coil).

faq.q: What milk works best for frothing?
faq.a: Whole milk produces the richest froth. Among plant milks, oat barista blend froths best.

faq.q: How do I clean the Aeroccino?
faq.a: Wash the interior and whisk with warm soapy water after each use. Never submerge the base unit.
